LAW AND MOTION 302,PLAINTIFFS MICAH VICKNER AND FRANK VICKNER'S MOTION TO STAY THIS ACTION PENDING THE CALIFORNIA SUPREME COURTS DECISION. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS UNOPPOSED. A GENERAL STAY OF THIS CASE IS IMPOSED. IT EXPIRES OF ITS OWN ACCORD ON THE DAY THE SUPREME COURT FILES IT DECISION IN RODRIGUEZ V. FCA US, LLC, S274625. ON THE PUBLICATION OF THE SUPREME COURT'S DECISION IN RODRIGUEZ, THE PARTIES MUST IMMEDIATELY FILE A STATEMENT, WITH A COPY TO DEPARTMENT 206 (DEPARTMENT OF THE PRESIDING JUDGE), STATING THAT THE STAY HAS EXPIRED OF ITS OWN ACCORD AND THAT THE CASE MAY BE SET DOWN FOR TRIAL. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM TO THE COURT'S RULING. JUDGE: CURTIS E.A. KARNOW, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (302/CK)
ORDER REGARDING PLAINTIFFS' MOTION TO STAY

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay This Action Pending The California Supreme Courts Decision
Off Calendar Dec-01-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFFS MICAH VICKNER and FRANK VICKNER's Motion To Stay This Action Pending The California Supreme Courts Decision.

The motion is granted as unopposed. A general stay of this case is imposed. It expires of its own accord on the day the Supreme Court files it decision in Rodriguez v. FCA US, LLC, S274625. On the publication of the Supreme court's decision in Rodriguez, the parties must immediately file a statement, with a copy to Department 206 (Department of the Presiding Judge), stating that the stay has expired of its own accord and that the case may be set down for trial.

Prevailing party to prepare a proposed order that is verbatim to the Court's ruling.

Judge: Curtis E.A. Karnow, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/CK)
